text: Writing clean code is essential for creating software that is easy to read and maintain. One of the most important practices is using clear naming conventions for variables, functions, and classes. Names should describe their purpose, making the code self-explanatory. Another key idea is to keep functions short and focused on a single task. This not only improves readability but also makes testing and debugging simpler. Well-structured code with small, reusable functions allows other developers to understand and modify it quickly. Ultimately, writing clean code saves time and effort in the long run, as it reduces confusion and errors, leading to more reliable and maintainable software.
